Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Continued To Jan-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Trustee.
Kathy Fong (Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.
Florence Chang (Kathy's Friend) also present.

Ms. Chang stated that Ms. Fong wants to know where the fees are being paid from. Ms. Ng stated that they are asking for fees for Special Counsel to come out of the Trust, that Ms. Fong is the income beneficiary of the Trust and that the fee is usually split 50/50. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-23-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Andrea Leung.
Jay Withee for and with Cathy Fong (Objector).
Deborah Barron appeared by telephone for Gordon Fong.
Hsin-Ju Chang Interpreter (Mandarin).

The Court inquired as to the certification status of the interpreter. The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Hsin-Ju Chang. Her Certification Number is 301206. She has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Mandarin to English and English to Mandarin.

Mr. Withee provided filed stamped copies of his Client's pleadings, stated that he filed evidentiary objections to Gordon's most recent submission, that it is still pending in e-file and that it was e-served. The Court stated that it is prepared to hear argument and take the matter under submission. Ms. Ng stated that the Trustee filed a response to the supplement on June 18th, that it included a letter for the corporate counsel, and that she is happy to submit on the pleadings. Ms. Barron stated that she submitted objections and declarations of the stock holders and the Children, that they have received all of their stock certificates and they have been submitted, and that a declaration from the Secretary who saw Mr. Fong sign them was provided. Mr. Withee withdraws his Client's evidentiary objections, stated that the arguments are laid out in the briefs, and that he is willing to submit on the papers and respond to any questions the Court may have. The Court stated that it has received evidence (stock certificates/dividends/ledger) from both sides, that it will review everything that has been presented and that it will make a decision. Ms. Ng and Ms. Barron are to work together and submit a proposed order that expresses their views. Mr. Withee is to submit a proposed order that expresses his view. The propose orders are to be e-mailed to the Court Clerk in the form of a word document and hard copies are to be provided to the Court by August 8th. Mr. Withee raised the issue of lodging the original ledger. Ms. Ng and Ms. Barron have no objection to the Court looking at copies of the ledger. The Second and Final Account and Report, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Continued To Aug-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Andrea Leung.
Deborah Barron appeared by telephone for Gordon Fong.
Jay Withee for and with Cathy Fong (Beneficiary).
Amanda Peeters Court Interpreter also present.

The Court inquired as to the certification status of the interpreter. The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Amanda Peeters. Her Certification Number is 301183. She has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Mandarin to English and English to Mandarin.

The Court noted that at the last hearing there was an issue of whether the Trust holds 64% or 20% of H & M Corporation and inquires as to whether the parties have talked about how they will be proceeding. Mr. Withee stated that at the last hearing his Client indicated that she is willing to mediate, but did not receive any responses. Ms. Barron stated that she is not willing to mediate, and that mediation will not work because it is a question of law and fact. Mr. Withee spoke of communication issues/language barriers with his Client, informed the Court that his Client has an original red ledger book of H & M Fong Company, stated that he would like to submit a supplemental declaration to authenticate the ledger and that he would like to present it to the Court as it addresses a factual issue. Mr. Withee also suggested serving it on Ms. Ng and Mr. Escobar, stated that he will then go from there to determine if an evidentiary hearing is needed and that he can not finalize his position until he confers with his Client in private. Ms. Barron stated that she has never seen this ledger. The Court instructed Counsel to meet and confer prior to the next hearing date with regard to how the case will proceed towards resolution (submit/trial and how long/mediation). The Second and Final Account and Report, etc. is continued to August 1, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Withee is to file a supplemental declaration with the materials now found. The Parties are to file a joint statement no later than July 25, 2018 with the results of the meet and confer as to how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosada. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Continued To Jun-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Andea Leung
Deborah Barron for Gordon Fong via CourtCall
Jay Withee for and with Cathy Fong

Mr. Withee requested an opportunity to respond to the auditor's report, that there is the legal question of whether the share certificates or tax documents take precedence, that his client is an elderly woman without resources counting on Trust income, thinks that mediation does work, and his client cannot afford her half of mediation costs. Ms. Ng stated that they want to conclude with the 2nd accounting, they are trying to do what is necessary as Trustee, and the Trustee will not take a position if parties go into mediation. Ms. Barron stated they are not refusing mediation if Trustee wants to mediate, she does not think mediation will resolve issues, she thought the legal issue was resolved by the expert who is a corporate attorney, and the agreement to re-purchase stocks no matter how much is there. The Second and Final Report and Account, etc. is continued to June 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Withee's responses to Trustee's recent filings to be filed by May 31, 2018. Ms. Barron and Ms. Ng's replies to Mr. Withee's responses to be filed by June 7, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Continued To May-23-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jay Withee for and with Cathy Fong
Lillian Ng for Andrea Leung
The Court inquired as to the certification status of the interpreter. The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Hsin-Ju Chang. Her Certification Number is 301206. She has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Mandarin to English and English to Mandarin.

The Court inquired about the process of going forward now that it has authorized hiring of an attorney. Ms. Ng stated that she has spoken with corporate Counsel, that she was told a request was provided to Ms. Barron and Mr. Withee for some documents, that they are at the information gathering stage at this point, requests a 30 day continuance to get results/comments from Counsel, and will then determine if she will need to amend the petition. Mr. Withee stated that his client will agree to a 30 day continuance, his client was hoping the Trustee would take a hand in resolving the dispute, applauds the Trustee for retaining the services of an auditor. Mr. Withee also stated his client wants this over with, his client has provided a lot of evidence that her late husband owned 64.17% interest of the company in the Trust, and she does not want it to go past 30 days. The Second and Final Report and Account, etc. is continued to May 23, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of where things are, with urging that investigation proceed, and the parties cooperate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Andrea Leung (Trustee).
Jay Withee for and with Cathy Fong (Beneficiary/Objector).
The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Bannie Chow. Her Certification Number is 100364. She has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Cantonese to English and English to Cantonese.

Mr. Withee stated that there is an issue with percentages of share holdings, that Ms. Ng is going to petition the Court for instructions as to the percentages, that there is an issue of validity of certain share transfers, that his Client does not accept the percentages (80% Children/20% Trust) at face value and that he would like to do some discovery. Mr. Withee also stated that he wanted Ms. Ng to do discovery, but there is a conflict because Gordon is a Beneficiary and that he would like 60-days. In addition, Mr. Withee stated that during Gordon Fong serving as Successor Trustee, he sold the Trust interest back to H & M Fong, Inc. for $270,000.00 which is less than what it was valued by the current Trustee, that he is asking for an accounting, that Ms. Barron is refusing to account and that there is a $630,000.00 loss to the Trust. The Second and Final Report and Account, etc. is continued to February 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties should be prepared to discuss whether mediation will be considered or where this is going.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-02-2017 Continued To Nov-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Andrea Leung (Former Successor Trustee).
Jay Withee for and with Cathy Fong (Beneficiary).

Mr. Withee stated that he just came into the case recently, that there is a dispute between Mrs. Fong and the Stepchildren as to the ownership percentages in the H & M Corporation (corporation that owns property in San Francisco) and that Mrs. Fong has evidence that her Husband owned 64.7 percent up to the point he no longer had capacity. Mr. Withee also stated that Mrs. Fong has tax returns from 2001 through 2013, dividend payments to Herbert and the 5 Children, and asked that this matter be bifurcated as they have evidence that it is not a 20/80 split. Ms. Ng stated that she would like to have Ms. Leung's final accounting as Temporary Trustee proceed and bifurcate the ownership issue, that they have a pending petition for general appointment of Ms. Leung on October 23rd, that she will be filing a petition for instructions and that perhaps the Court could approve or allow the accounting without the H & M stocks. Mr. Withee stated that Mrs. Fong acknowledges that Ms. Leung has authority to address this issue. The Court indicated that it does not make sense to bifurcate the issue of the H & M ownership percentages. The Second and Final Account and Report, etc. is continued to November 13, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Withee to file his objections and for Ms. Ng to file her reply.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatees Trust To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee And For The Appointment Of Success Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with (petitioner) Andrea Leung. Deborah Barron for and with (petitioner) Gordon Fong.

The Court stated that at the last hearing it was told that Ms. Leung would be withdrawing her petition and asked what has changed. Ms. Ng stated that she spoke with Ms. Leung, that the background of the property is that it is a mess due to what Mr. Fong and his wife did or did not do and that with Ms. Leung as conservator is seems that it would be easier for her to straighten things out. Ms. Barron stated that Gordon is his father's choice, that he has been managing the properties and was doing so prior to the appointment of the temporary trustee, that he is capable of completing an accounting with attorney assistance, that there is a waiver of bond on file, that there are some things to straighten out such as the list of assets as well as stock that needs to be added to the trust or bought out and that the family feels that Gordon is the best choice. Ms. Ng stated that Ms. Leung works closely with the family members with the exception of Kathy, that this would be temporary to get things straightened out, that it might make it easier for everyone, that they need professional property management, that the tenants have the run of the property, that they need to coordinate between the trust and conservatorship assets, that they possibly need to sell the property and that as far as a timeline the first accounting would let them know where things stand. Ms. Barron stated that the property must be sold, that there are not funds to do the earthquake retrofitting required by the City, that they have a short window of time to do so and that Gordon would be able to give the court any necessary reports it needs. The two petitions for court supervision of conservatees trust to confirm vacancy in the office of trustee and for the appointment of successor trustee were taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)
